title: Objects, Classes, Namespaces, and Binding · Mastery Check
summary: Model a library circulation domain while tracing real Python object and attribute behavior. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an inspectable library domain model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reason about object identity, type, value, mutability, equality, and lifetime in an object-oriented design.
Define classes and create instances while distinguishing class objects from the instances they construct.
Trace class, instance, module, and local namespaces during attribute lookup and assignment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-oop cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Oop Class Static Methods applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.
